# The Digital Heir

> The Digital Heir is a secure digital inheritance service for protecting and transferring sensitive digital information only when the owner becomes unavailable.

The service allows users to create an encrypted digital Envelope, add trusted heirs, configure secret questions, verify contact channels, and build an inactivity Pipeline that can release a limited-time decryption link to the selected heir.

## Product summary

The Digital Heir helps users protect sensitive digital assets such as passwords, private keys, recovery phrases, account access instructions, confidential notes, and important personal information.

The core idea is simple: the user prepares encrypted information in advance, defines who should receive access, and configures the conditions under which delivery should happen.

## Security model

- Sensitive Envelope content is encrypted before storage.
- Secret answers are not stored as plain text.
- The service is designed to avoid early disclosure to heirs.
- The heir receives access only if the configured inactivity Pipeline reaches its final step.
- Access is protected through secret questions and anti-brute-force controls.

## Public repository
